title: Nephrodium tomentosum
intro: Nephrodium tomentosum, also known as the hairy maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium tomentosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Nephrodium tomentosum belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, which is a division of plants known as Pteridophyta. The genus Nephrodium is now considered a synonym of Dryopteris. The classification of Nephrodium tomentosum is still used in some contexts, particularly in older botanical literature. Its classification as a whole is a part of the larger kingdom Plantae.
uses: Nephrodium tomentosum is known for its potential medicinal uses, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively studied. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could be useful in treating various health conditions. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects. Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal benefits of Nephrodium tomentosum.

parts: Nephrodium tomentosum, also known as the tomentose nephroid, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine.
